original_abstract: Due to their diverse and potent biological profiles, quinazoline derivatives have become a focal point of contemporary synthetic chemistry and pharmacological research. This review provides a comprehensive overview of recent advancements in the synthesis and bioactivity of these N-containing heterocycles. We categorize current synthetic strategies into five primary methodologies: Aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal-mediated processes, ultrasound-promoted reactions, and phase-transfer catalysis. Additionally, the review highlights the therapeutic potential of synthesized quinazoline derivatives, exploring their roles as anti-cancer agents, anti-inflammatory drugs, and antimicrobials, among other medicinal applications. Through this analysis, we aim to provide a valuable reference for the future design and development of more potent quinazoline-based therapeutic agents.
humanized_abstract: Quinazoline derivatives owing to their diverse and vibrant bioactivity have gained immense interest to the researchers in the field of synthetic organic and drug research. Here in this review article we are reporting the updated literature survey on the synthesis and bioactivity of N- heterocycles quinazoline derivatives by dividing the recent synthesis methods into five classes i.e Aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal-mediated processes, ultrasound-promoted reactions and phase-transfer catalysis. Also, the reported literature survey revealed that the synthesized quinazoline derivatives have significant applications in the fields of anticancer, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial etc. So the main objective of this review article is to provide update literature which will help in designing new quinazoline derivatives to afford novel bioactivities in the realm of medicines.
